sections.paragraph:
ἄνιπτος, ον (α- priv., νίπτω; Hom. et al.) unwashed χεῖρες (Il. 6, 266; Philo, Spec. Leg. 2, 6) Mt 15:20. κοιναῖς χερσίν, τουτέστιν ἀνίπτοις Mk 7:2, 5 v.l. Acc. to a rabb. rule, going beyond the Torah, it was necessary to wash one’s hands before a meal; see Schürer II 477.—DELG s.v. νίπτω. TW.

priv. priv. = privativum (in grammar: privative, w. negating force)

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Il. Il. = Iliad, s. Hom.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

rabb. rabb. = rabbinical (literature etc.)

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 83
